subtitle: A 150-meter sheltered cove on Mykonos that a single beach club, Nammos, turned into the most expensive sand in Greece.
spike_statement: Psarou is a small south-coast Mykonos cove where Nammos rebuilt the shoreline into a luxury economy — and where Greece came to enforce who owns the beach.

levers.photo_tone: calm Aegean turquoise over a sheltered bay, white loungers in dense rows, moored yachts on the horizon line
